message:
feat(rust): Add taint analysis metadata for future implementation Add comprehensive metadata arrays for Rust taint analysis to establish the rule schema and documentation framework before implementing the actual detection logic. This follows the established pattern from JavaScript, Python, and Go modules. Metadata structure added: TAINT_RULE_IDS array (3 critical categories): - rust.taint.xss: XSS/HTML injection vulnerabilities - rust.taint.sql: SQL injection through string concatenation - rust.taint.command: Command injection via process execution TAINT_SUMMARY associative array: Maps each rule ID to a concise description of the vulnerability class: - [rust.taint.xss]: 'User input flows into HttpResponse/body/output macros without escaping' * Covers Actix-web HttpResponse::body(), Rocket responders, println!, format! macros that write to HTTP responses * Rust's type system prevents many XSS vectors but string interpolation with format! or direct body writes still vulnerable - [rust.taint.sql]: 'User input concatenated into SQL statements/executions' * Applies to Diesel raw SQL queries, sqlx::query! macro with interpolated strings, rusqlite execute() with format! concatenation * Rust's compile-time SQL checking (sqlx) helps but developers can still bypass with raw/dynamic query construction - [rust.taint.command]: 'User input reaches std::process::Command' * Detects flows into Command::new() or Command::arg() where untrusted input could inject additional arguments or commands * Particularly dangerous when .arg() receives unsanitized shell metacharacters TAINT_REMEDIATION associative array: Provides ecosystem-specific guidance for each vulnerability class: - [rust.taint.xss]: 'Escape template context (html_escape::encode_safe, askama filters) before writing responses' * Recommends html_escape crate for manual escaping * Suggests Askama template engine with auto-escaping enabled * Advises using typed response builders that enforce escaping - [rust.taint.sql]: 'Use parameterized queries (diesel/sqlx placeholders) instead of format! concatenation' * Diesel: Use .bind() parameters or query_builder with placeholders * sqlx: Use $1, $2 style placeholders in query! macro * rusqlite: Use ? placeholders with params! macro * Never use format! to construct SQL strings - [rust.taint.command]: 'Validate / whitelist args and avoid shell invocation when spawning commands' * Use Command::arg() separately for each argument instead of shell string * Validate inputs against allowlists (path canonicalization, regex patterns) * Avoid .env("SHELL", ...) or shell=true patterns * Consider using Command::args(&[...]) with pre-validated arguments TAINT_SEVERITY mapping: All three taint rule categories marked as 'critical' severity since they represent direct paths to security compromise: - rust.taint.xss: critical (enables arbitrary JavaScript execution) - rust.taint.sql: critical (enables database manipulation/exfiltration) - rust.taint.command: critical (enables remote code execution) Rust-specific considerations: Type safety benefits: - Rust's ownership system prevents many injection classes by design - String interpolation requires explicit formatting, making flows more visible - Compile-time guarantees mean some taint flows are impossible (buffer overflows) Remaining vulnerabilities: Despite Rust's safety guarantees, taint analysis is still essential because: 1. format! and format_args! macros can construct malicious strings at runtime 2. Diesel/sqlx raw SQL escapes compile-time checking 3. Command::arg() accepts arbitrary strings without validation 4. HTTP response bodies are untyped byte streams 5. Many web frameworks use String types that can carry tainted data Common Rust patterns to detect: - format!("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {}", user_input) - HttpResponse::Ok().body(format!("
{}
", comment)) - Command::new("sh").arg(format!("-c ls {}", path)) - query.push_str(&format!("AND name = '{}'", name)) Implementation plan: This metadata commit is the first step toward Rust taint analysis support. Future commits will add: 1. run_taint_analysis_checks() helper function with Python analyzer script - Source patterns: std::env::args(), actix_web::web::Query, rocket::form::Form, warp::query(), axum::extract::Query - Sink patterns: HttpResponse::body, Response::body, println!/format! to response, query/execute methods, Command::new/arg - Sanitizer patterns: html_escape::encode_safe, askama auto-escape, diesel bind parameters, sqlx placeholders 2. Test fixtures demonstrating vulnerable and secure patterns: - test-suite/rust/buggy/taint_analysis.rs with Actix-web handlers - test-suite/rust/clean/taint_analysis.rs with proper escaping 3. Manifest test cases for regression coverage: - rust-taint-buggy case expecting nonzero exit and critical findings - rust-taint-clean case expecting zero exit and no critical findings 4. Documentation updates in test-suite/README.md and main README.md This metadata-first approach allows incremental development while maintaining clear documentation of the intended rule semantics and remediation guidance. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude

files.patch:
@@ -197,6 +197,24 @@ declare -A ASYNC_ERROR_SEVERITY=(
   [rust.async.tokio-task-no-await]='warning'
 )
 
+# Taint analysis metadata
+TAINT_RULE_IDS=(rust.taint.xss rust.taint.sql rust.taint.command)
+declare -A TAINT_SUMMARY=(
+  [rust.taint.xss]='User input flows into HttpResponse/body/output macros without escaping'
+  [rust.taint.sql]='User input concatenated into SQL statements/executions'
+  [rust.taint.command]='User input reaches std::process::Command'
+)
+declare -A TAINT_REMEDIATION=(
+  [rust.taint.xss]='Escape template context (html_escape::encode_safe, askama filters) before writing responses'
+  [rust.taint.sql]='Use parameterized queries (diesel/sqlx placeholders) instead of format! concatenation'
+  [rust.taint.command]='Validate / whitelist args and avoid shell invocation when spawning commands'
+)
+declare -A TAINT_SEVERITY=(
+  [rust.taint.xss]='critical'
+  [rust.taint.sql]='critical'
+  [rust.taint.command]='critical'
+)
+
 # Resource lifecycle correlation spec (acquire vs release pairs)
 RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE_IDS=(thread_join tokio_spawn tcp_shutdown)
 declare -A RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE_SEVERITY=(
